task_completion.description: Verify that the Sygic GPS Navigation application is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sygic GPS Navigation app interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent searches for the correct destination.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar or search results in the Sygic app show 'San Jose, CA' or 'San Jose'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a driving route is planned to San Jose, CA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sygic app displays a route summary or map showing a calculated driving route ending in San Jose, CA.

task_completion.description: Verify that the estimated trip duration is displayed on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A duration string (e.g., '34 min', '1 h 12 min') is clearly visible on the Sygic route summary screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent targets the correct output file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., using 'echo' or 'cat') or a text editor is shown targeting the exact file path '/sdcard/trip_duration.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the estimated trip duration is recorded in the output file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terminal or text editor shows the exact duration string (matching the one seen in the Sygic app) being written to '/sdcard/trip_duration.txt'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the actual Sygic GPS Navigation app to find the route.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Sygic app interface (com.sygic.aura), not a web browser, Google Maps, or another navigation tool.

integrity.description: Verify that the recorded duration is authentic and not hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The duration value written to the text file must exactly match the duration displayed in the Sygic app route summary screenshot.

integrity.description: Verify that the output file contains ONLY the duration string.
integrity.visual_evidence: The command or text editor writing to the file shows only the duration string (e.g., '1 h 12 min') without any extra words, explanations, or conversational text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Destination: San Jose, CA. Output file path: /sdcard/trip_duration.txt. The agent must use the Sygic GPS Navigation app to calculate the route. The target UI element is the estimated duration string (e.g., '34 min', '1 h 12 min') displayed on the Sygic route summary screen. The output file must contain ONLY this exact duration string.
